Vision, Mission & Core Values To become a world-class University that contributes significantly to the development ofregional economy and uplift of local community by becoming a power house of intellectualand human capital generation. Financial Aid In order to facilitate the talented and needy student, the university offers substantial number of scholarships and financial assistants so that the future leaders do not discontinue their studies because of financial constraints. Admissions 94 programs offered 28 Engineering Degree Programs 10 CS& IT Degree Programs Admissions Admission to Khwaja Fareed University of Engineering and Information Technology (KFUEIT) is based on merit and is open for various undergraduate (BS), graduate (MS), and doctoral (PhD) programs. Applicants must meet specific academic requirements and pass the university’s entry test, followed by an interview. The university typically announces admissions for different programs once or twice a year, and interested candidates can apply online through the official KFUEIT website or by visiting the admissions office. ICT Facilities ICT infrastructure has been developed, based on the cloud computing paradigm. Keeping in view the security of information and requirements of a teaching and research university, both private as well as public clouds are leveraged.

Views of Our Students Syed Hafeez Kazmi BS Mechanical Engineering It's an honour for me to be a part of the Institute of Mechanical and Manufacturing Engineering. It's the best platform ever where you find learning with fun, entertainment, culture and manay other life preaching activities. The most important thing is grooming for professional life. M. Hamza Babar Ms Zoology My experience MS Zoology student in the life sciences department at KFUEIT has been incredibly rewarding. The department of Life Sciences has provided me with a deep understanding of zoological concept and methodologies. The faculy members have been supportive and knowledgeable, guiding me through my academic journey. Previous Next Views of Our Alumni Jareer Ahmed BS Food Science and Technology Hello, I’m Jareer Ahmed, a proud alumnus of BS Food Science and Technology (Batch 2018-2022), KFUEIT. My time at KFUEIT provided invaluable knowledge and practical skills. Currently, I work as Assistant Manager Supply Planning Dairy at FrieslandCampina Engro Pakistan Limited. Hamza Akram BS Computer Science I'm Hamza Akram, a proud alumnus of KFUEIT (Computer Science, 2018-2022). I am honored to have been selected for the SUSI for Student Leaders program at the University of Massachusetts. My journey at KFUEIT has been transformative, and I’m grateful for the opportunities that have shaped my leadership path. Previous Next Views of Our External Guests Dr. Baptiste Chatre Project Head GIZ Pakistan This is my first visit to South Punjab and KFUEIT. I was impressed by the campus facilities, especially the auditorium, sports complex, green spaces, and solar panel installations at parking spots. I'm surprised and happy to see KFUEIT’s progress. Dr. Claudia Ringler Director Natural Resources and Resilience, IFPRI I spent a week at this exceptional university, learning how to collaborate with farmers, private sector companies, scientists, and students to improve water, food, energy, and environmental sustainability in Southern Punjab. The university stands out, especially for its large female faculty. Previous Next

Home General News Huge Congratulations to the winner of “Entrepreneurial Female Youth Pitch Competition”
Huge Congratulations to the winner of “Entrepreneurial Female Youth Pitch Competition”
Huge Congratulations to the winner of “Entrepreneurial Female Youth Pitch Competition” General News News & Updates KFUEIT is proud to recognize the outstanding achievement of Ms. Faiza Amir Khakwani, a MBA 4th Semester student, who recently secured the grant of 1 million by winning Entrepreneurial Female Youth Pitch Competition, organized by Women Development Department, Gov of Punjab. The successful pitch of a startup is a significant undertaking, and Ms. Faiza Amir Khakwani demonstrated exceptional strategic thinking and presentation skills. This victory highlights the talent and drive fostered within KFUEIT, Rahim Yar Khan. The university also commends the efforts of Dr. Sidra Ghazanfar, Focal Person of “Entrepreneurial Female Youth Pitch Competition”, for her dedicated mentorship and guidance. The “Entrepreneurial Female Youth Pitch Competition” was initiated by Women Development Department, Gov of Punjab to encourage female university students across Punjab to transform their innovative ideas into sustainable businesses. By providing a structured pathway ranging from idea pitching and mentorship to seed funding and follow-up support the program directly aligns with WDD’s mission to empower women as drivers of economic growth, job creation, and community development.

Home Events KFUEIT Book Fair & Literary Festival 2026
KFUEIT Book Fair & Literary Festival 2026
KFUEIT Book Fair & Literary Festival 2026 Events General News Description Immerse yourself in a world of stories, ideas, and imagination at the KFUEIT Book Fair & Literary Festival 2026 . Taking place on May 6–7, 2026 , starting at 11:00 AM , this two-day event transforms the Civil Building into a hub of creativity and intellectual engagement. Organized by the KFUEIT Central Library in collaboration with the KFUEIT Readers’ & Writers’ Club , the festival offers a unique opportunity to explore a vast collection of books and participate in literary activities designed to inspire. Whether you are a dedicated bibliophile or a casual reader, we invite you to join the campus community in celebrating the power of literature and the enduring impact of the written word.

record_payload.title: https://kfueit.edu.pk/official-visit-of-chairman-hec-to-kfueit-strengthening-research-innovation-and-sustainability
record_payload.content:
Home General News Official Visit of Chairman HEC to KFUEIT: Strengthening Research, Innovation, and Sustainability
Official Visit of Chairman HEC to KFUEIT: Strengthening Research, Innovation, and Sustainability
Official Visit of Chairman HEC to KFUEIT: Strengthening Research, Innovation, and Sustainability General News News & Updates The visit of Prof. Dr. Niaz Ahmad Akhtar (Sitara-i-Imtiaz) , Chairman of the Higher Education Commission (HEC) Pakistan, to Khwaja Fareed University of Engineering & Information Technology (KFUEIT) on April 24, 2026, marked a significant milestone in the university's journey toward academic excellence. The Chairman was received with a warm and cordial welcome by Vice Chancellor Prof. Ir. Dr. Amir Azam Khan, Registrar Engr. Prof. Dr. Javed Iqbal, and senior faculty members, symbolizing the university’s honor in hosting such distinguished leadership. During his visit, the Chairman inaugurated two pioneering research facilities: the Khwaja Fareed Environmental Testing Laboratories , which is the first EPA-certified facility of its kind in South Punjab, and the Center for Insect Farming and Entomological Entrepreneurship (CIFEE) . These facilities are designed to address environmental challenges through accurate testing and promote sustainable agriculture through innovative research on alternative protein sources. The visit also included a comprehensive tour of the university's modern infrastructure, where the Chairman inspected advanced engineering workshops, IT labs, and research centers. He delivered an inspiring lecture to the faculty, emphasizing the transformative role of universities in national development and the importance of aligning with global trends in emerging fields such as Artificial Intelligence, Cyber Security, and Robotics. Furthermore, the Chairman participated in a tree plantation ceremony to reaffirm the university's commitment to environmental sustainability and engaged with the media to highlight the critical role of higher education in driving innovation and research across Pakistan.

record_payload.content:
Home Events 2nd Industrial Open House, Career Fair & Project Expo 2026 at KFUEIT
2nd Industrial Open House, Career Fair & Project Expo 2026 at KFUEIT
2nd Industrial Open House, Career Fair & Project Expo 2026 at KFUEIT Events General News The Directorate of Career Counselling and Industrial Placement at Khwaja Fareed University of Engineering & Information Technology (KFUEIT), in collaboration with ORIC and the Alumni Association, is honored to invite your esteemed organization to the 2nd Industrial Open House, Career Fair & Project Expo 2026 . This flagship event, scheduled for May 12–13, 2026 , serves as a premier bridge between academia and industry, fostering a dynamic environment for recruitment, innovation, and professional networking. Attendees will have the opportunity to engage with high-caliber talent through Final Year Design Project exhibitions , explore recruitment prospects at the Career Fair , and participate in panel discussions featuring distinguished C-suite executives and industry leaders. We invite you to set up a dedicated recruitment or awareness stall to directly interact with our skilled student body and showcase your organization’s vision. Event Details: Dates: 12–13 May, 2026 Time: 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM Venue: KFUEIT Campus, Rahim Yar Khan Contact for Registration: yasir.niaz@kfueit.edu.pk | +92 335 3420890 We look forward to your participation in strengthening academia–industry linkages and shaping the future of Pakistan's industrial landscape.

Scholarships and Financial Assistance Department
Scholarships and Financial Assistance Department Dr. Muhammad Aslam Khan Director Scholarships and Financial Assistance Tel / Ext. : +92 (68) 588 2479 Email: dsfa@kfueit.edu.pk Dr. Rana Mujahid Incharge Scholarships and Financial Assistance Tel / Ext. : +92 34504292460 Email: rmujahidsaleem@kfueit.edu.pk In order to facilitate the talented, needy, low-income, marginalised or refugee students, the university provides a substantial number of scholarships and financial assistants for their university fee, hostel/accommodation and living allowance so that future leaders do not discontinue their studies because of financial constraints. The scholarships are also offered at the time of admission so that any prejudice againt the poor, low-income neighbourhoods, class, creed or race is removed. To ensure transparency and meritocracy, a committee manages all matters related to scholarships and financial assistance. The major function of the Scholarships Committee is not only to evaluate the Need Based and Merit Scholarships but also to recommend the students for Fareed Scholarship, Pakistan Baitul Mal, Ministry of Religious Affairs, and IEP – SAC (Saudi Arabia) Scholarship. KFUEIT Free Medical Camp: Committed to a Healthier Community
KFUEIT Free Medical Camp: Committed to a Healthier Community Events General News Khwaja Fareed University of Engineering & Information Technology (KFUEIT) is proud to announce a Free Medical Camp on April 29, 2026 , bringing essential healthcare services directly to your doorstep. This initiative aims to foster a healthier campus and community by providing expert medical consultations, basic diagnostic tests, and comprehensive health screenings at absolutely no cost. We invite all students, faculty, and community members to take advantage of this valuable opportunity to prioritize their well-being and benefit from professional medical guidance in a supportive and accessible environment.

record_payload.title: https://kfueit.edu.pk/vision-mission-and-core-values
record_payload.content:
Home Vision, Mission and Core Values
Vision, Mission and Core Values
Vision, Mission and Core Values Our Vision To become a World Class University that contributes significantly for the development of regional economy to uplift local community by becoming a power house of intellectuals and human capital generation. Our Mission To offer an educational experience wherein: The curriculum and its delivery confirm to international standards The students are provided an environment for wholesome development of their personality and creative potential The graduates produced are most sought after by prospective employers To conduct research to solve local and national problems requiring knowledge based solutions. Goals & Objectives Establish 30 world-class undergraduate degree programs in six domains of Sciences & Mathematics, Engineering, Information & Communication Technology, Life Sciences & Healthcare, Management, Social & Legal Sciences, Humanities & Arts. Conduct research in inter-disciplinary areas of local and national need to produce intellectual capital (knowledge, solutions, Intellectual Property (IP)) and human capital (PhDs): Applied & Open Source Information Technology (Education, Health and Agriculture) Embedded Systems (Next Generation Smartphones, Tablets, Security Cameras, Home Automation Systems, Building Controls Systems, Factory & Production Line Automation for Agri-based Industries, Electric Vehicles Control Systems) Biotechnology (better crops and fruit varieties) Food Science & Technology Water Resource Engineering / Management Environmental Science / Engineering Renewable Energy (Biomass, Solar PV, Wind and others) Information Security (Information Assurance, Cyber Warfare, Digital Forensics) Materials Engineering (Solid Electrolytes, Organic Photovoltaics, Nano-materials) Power Storage, Conversion & Management Heritage Studies Engineering Management, Healthcare Management and Supply Chain Management Our Core Values At Fareed University of Engineering & Information Technology, Rahim Yar Khan we have chosen a set of core values which fully embody our vision and mission. These core values permeate all our activities and form a benchmark to judge our efforts against. These core values are the qualities that we want to maximize in our students, faculty and staff. Furthermore, we believe that these core values will allow us to transform our society into an advance one. Our core values are based on our acronym so that they become easy for us to remember. A look at the shades of meaning conveyed by these core values will help us understand fully the impactthat we intend these core values to have on ourstudents, faculty and staff:

record_payload.title: https://kfueit.edu.pk/why-kfueit
record_payload.content:
Why KFUEIT Apply Now A Premium Institution in Making The KFUEIT Rahim Yar Khan is the first university in a radius of 200 km embarking upon its plans to spread quality education in a highly professional environment with grace, pride and dignity. This institution is nothing lesser than a blessing for the area of Rahim Khan in particular and Southern Punjab and Northern Sindh in general. Looking at its aggressive expansion plans w.r.t. infrastructural development, hiring talented teaching and non-teaching staff, adding new programs in academic portfolio, building systems & policies and ensuring professional environment are evident of the fact that “A Premium Institution is in Making”. Professional Environment We promote academic excellence through professional environment at KFUEIT where students are treated with respect and care while having certain level of freedom. Our culture and employees are catalyst in ensuring an environment where students are provided with numerous opportunities to learn, grow and at the same time make their stay at this university enjoyable and life time experience. State of Art Infrastructure & Facilities The current area of KFUEIT in Phase-1 is 279 acres and with acquisition of 200 acres more land in Phase-2 the total area sums up to 479 acres. The substantial infrastructural development of first phase has given university a great shape and with the completion of phase 2, this institution will stand out amongst top institutes of the country. The development, facilities and systems being installed here are state of the art, while laying foundation with futuristic approach. The university stands out with Hi-tech ICT System, automation and digitalization Well-equipped and modernized security set up Automated and computerized admission, testing and hiring processes Departmental buildings and class rooms equipped with latest technologies Spacious auditorium and Library Providing wide range of sports infrastructure and facilities Hostel facility for boys and girls Environmental friendly campus with dedicated horticulture department Development of Specialized and Transferrable Skills Education at KFUEIT allows you to get exposed to various technical subjects and skillset where you will be able to develop desired aspects of your traits and polish soft skills along with academic laurels. Students learn here the skills that are transferable to most industries such as communications, decision making, problem solving, project management, innovation and teamwork. The students of KFUEIT will have the knowledge to contribute to a rapidly changing technological environment. Personal Development Opportunities During their course of studies, our students come across with numerous opportunities within and outside the university to learn and develop what it takes to become a better human being and successful professional. Many curricular and extracurricular activities are arranged by the university whereas many more are initiated and arranged by students themselves. These project and activities give multidimensional platforms to students in improving their soft skills, build more networking among university colleagues and industry stakeholders. Career Oriented Education In order to make the students prepared for upcoming challenges and requirements of the practical world, KFUEIT organizes regular educational trips and internships. Our Career Counselling and Industrial Placement Office plays an instrumental role in shaping up student’s future plans and educational aspirations by inviting guest speakers from relevant industries, arranging industrial visits, conducting mock interviews, recommendations for internships and job placements. Extracurricular Activities The university provides a perfect blend of academic and extracurricular activities, because we believe in grooming our students by getting them exposed to number of real World scenarios. The events and activities like sports week, body building competition, women pink day, declamation competitions, Mushaira / bait baazi, fun fairs, cultural days, welcome & farewell parties are adding colors to the campus life. Our students have won many laurels for the university.

Spacious auditorium and Library
Providing wide range of sports infrastructure and facilities
Hostel facility for boys and girls
Environmental friendly campus with dedicated horticulture department
Development of Specialized and Transferrable Skills
Education at KFUEIT allows you to get exposed to various technical subjects and skillset where you will be able to develop desired aspects of your traits and polish soft skills along with academic laurels. Students learn here the skills that are transferable to most industries such as communications, decision making, problem solving, project management, innovation and teamwork. The students of KFUEIT will have the knowledge to contribute to a rapidly changing technological environment.
Personal Development Opportunities
During their course of studies, our students come across with numerous opportunities within and outside the university to learn and develop what it takes to become a better human being and successful professional. Many curricular and extracurricular activities are arranged by the university whereas many more are initiated and arranged by students themselves. These project and activities give multidimensional platforms to students in improving their soft skills, build more networking among university colleagues and industry stakeholders.
Career Oriented Education
In order to make the students prepared for upcoming challenges and requirements of the practical world, KFUEIT organizes regular educational trips and internships. Our Career Counselling and Industrial Placement Office plays an instrumental role in shaping up student’s future plans and educational aspirations by inviting guest speakers from relevant industries, arranging industrial visits, conducting mock interviews, recommendations for internships and job placements.
Extracurricular Activities
The university provides a perfect blend of academic and extracurricular activities, because we believe in grooming our students by getting them exposed to number of real World scenarios. The events and activities like sports week, body building competition, women pink day, declamation competitions, Mushaira / bait baazi, fun fairs, cultural days, welcome & farewell parties are adding colors to the campus life. Our students have won many laurels for the university.

Home Campus Life Graduates Welcomed by TANG International Group China
Graduates Welcomed by TANG International Group China
Graduates Welcomed by TANG International Group China Campus Life KFUEIT Graduates Welcomed by TANG International Group, China A grand reception ceremony was held in Jinzhou, China, to honor the graduates of Khwaja Fareed University of Engineering and Information Technology (KFUEIT). Dignitaries from Tang International Group graced the occasion and warmly welcomed the fifty-member delegation from KFUEIT. KFUEIT Vice-Chancellor delivered a special video message, extending congratulations to the students for their opportunity to pursue education and employment prospects in China. He highlighted the significant achievement of KFUEIT students in securing training and employment opportunities in China, emphasizing their potential contribution to the national economy through global platforms. Dr. Murtaza underlined that such initiatives underscore the robust friendship between Pakistan and China. Officials from Tang International Group also expressed their best wishes, and tokens of appreciation were exchanged during the event. This collaboration symbolizes the strong ties between Pakistan and China and demonstrates the global recognition of KFUEIT's graduates.

record_payload.title: https://kfueit.edu.pk/student-facilitation-center
record_payload.content:
Home Campus Life Student Facilitation Center
Student Facilitation Center
Student Facilitation Center Campus Life The Student Facilitation Service Centre (SFSC) has been established to facilitate KFUEIT students in their academic and non-academic issues under one roof. This department provides guidance and requisite information to the students under one roof. Students submit all their applications/queries to the KFUEIT Student administration department by using a Student Facilitation Service Centre Online token generation System. By using this System, students submit their various applications/queries which include those related to routine academic issues related to the following Departments ICT Department Examination Department Registrar Department Finance Department Hostel Department Scholarship Department Student Affairs Department Admission Department Career Counseling Department The user-friendly interface of the SFSC portal allows students to generate tokens and track their applications/queries, review the applications and update the Application Status along with their comments which can also be viewed by students through email and LMS portal. One way to assess the performance of a student facilitation center is to track the number of students who use its services. Since its inception in June 2021, more than 170,000 visits have been made by students and submitted their queries which were effectively and timely resolved. Our Mission By keeping equity at the center of our work, the facilitation Centre is motivated to provide our students with the highest standard service possible through a lens of inclusion and compassion. Facilitation Centre is committed in supporting student success by providing high quality, timely, and clear communication to our students. Our Values Compassion Facilitation Centre practices empathy, respect, professionalism, kindness, and forgiveness in serving our students and with each other. Commitment At Facilitation Centre aim to do our best, always, and are driven to achieve excellence by valuing accountability, teamwork, and serving our students honorably. Communication At the Facilitation Centre we provide consistent, timely, and clear information to our students, and we ensure follow-through with actionable steps. Integrity We speak with integrity with each other and with our students. We practice direct communication and active listening to ease misunderstandings. Accountability We are transparent with our data, hold ourselves accountable, and alter our course of action through a comprehensive continuous improvement process.

record_payload.content:
Home Campus Life Project Exhibition Showcases
Project Exhibition Showcases
Project Exhibition Showcases Campus Life Art & Project Exhibition Final Year Project Exhibition Showcases Innovation at KFUEIT The Institute of Mechanical and Manufacturing Engineering at Khwaja Fareed University of Engineering and Information Technology (KFUEIT) recently hosted its eagerly awaited Final Year Project Exhibition, spotlighting the culmination of students' hard work and innovation. The distinguished Vice Chancellor, Prof. Dr. Shahzad Murtaza, inaugurated the exhibition. Throughout the event, he attentively observed the diverse array of projects presented by the students, praising their efforts, creativity, and dedication. The Vice Chancellor also extended his appreciation to the organizers for their meticulous planning and execution. The exhibition provided a platform for students to showcase their technical prowess, problem-solving abilities, and innovative ideas. It offered an invaluable opportunity for industry professionals, faculty members, and guests to witness the ingenuity and potential of the future engineers graduating from KFUEIT. The Institute of Mechanical and Manufacturing Engineering remains steadfast in its commitment to nurturing a culture of innovation and excellence, empowering students to emerge as leaders in their respective fields. Alongside Prof. Dr. Shahzad Murtaza, esteemed faculty members including Dr. Shahid Attique, Dr. Naeem Ullah Anmool, Dr. Muhammad Umar, and Dr. Anwar Farooq graced the occasion with their presence.

Inter-Departmental Sports Competitions Campus Life Department of Agricultural Engineering News & Events Sports & Fitness KFUEIT Hosts Exciting Inter-Departmental Sports Competitions The Directorate of Sports at Khwaja Fareed University of Engineering and Information Technology (KFUEIT) organized thrilling cricket matches and Tug of War competitions, bringing together students for a series of energetic events. Both male and female students from the Departments of Physical Education and Humanities participated in cricket matches, while the Departments of Agriculture and Physical Education competed in Tug of War contests, with separate events for male and female students. These activities created a vibrant and positive atmosphere on campus, encouraging teamwork, sportsmanship, and a strong sense of community among participants. The use of the university's scenic circle ground added to the excitement, making the experience memorable for everyone involved. Initiatives like these play a vital role in promoting physical activity, camaraderie, and student engagement, further enhancing the university's commitment to holistic student development.

record_payload.content:
Home Campus Life Art and Design Exhibition
Art and Design Exhibition
Art and Design Exhibition Campus Life Art & Project Exhibition Today, Khwaja Fareed University of Engineering and Information Technology witnessed a vibrant showcase of talent at its Art and Design Exhibition. Vice Chancellor, Professor Dr. Shahzad Murtaza, inaugurated the event, joined by distinguished guests including Dr. Ahmed Sohaib and Dr. Muhammad Anwar Farooq from the Institute of Humanities and Arts. From intricate paintings to innovative textile designs, students from various semesters displayed nearly 300 captivating art pieces. The exhibition, a testament to the university's commitment to holistic education, featured a diverse range of creations including calligraphy, landscaping, and Islamic product design, reflecting both traditional heritage and modern sensibilities. Speaking to the media, Professor Dr. Murtaza underscored the university's mission to nurture talent and instill practical skills alongside academic knowledge. He emphasized the importance of such platforms in honing students' abilities to meet the evolving needs of society. Applauding the efforts of the departments and organizers, especially the Institute of Humanities and Arts, Professor Dr. Murtaza reiterated the significance of regular exhibitions in aligning with the university's vision and providing students with valuable opportunities for growth. The event, attended by Course Coordinators and a multitude of students, not only celebrated creativity but also fostered a spirit of collaboration and appreciation among the university community.

record_payload.title: https://kfueit.edu.pk/transport-1
record_payload.content:
Home Campus Life Transport
Transport Campus Life The transport department renders the quality policy of KFUEIT by providing an unperturbed and cost-effective transport facility to students, faculty and staff. University has its own fleet of buses that includes 20 HINO/ISUZU Buses, one Toyota Coaster, one Toyota Hi-Ace, one Ambulance and other pool vehicles. Pickup and drop-off services are available for locations within the city and its surrounding areas within a 100 km radius. Moreover, transport is also provided to students for outdoor activities, sightseeing, picnic, excursion, study and field tour etc.

record_payload.title: https://kfueit.edu.pk/ai-expo-in-multan
record_payload.content:
Home Happenings At KFUEIT 𝐀𝐫𝐭𝐢𝐟𝐢𝐜𝐢𝐚𝐥 𝐈𝐧𝐭𝐞𝐥𝐥𝐢𝐠𝐞𝐧𝐜𝐞 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐈𝐧𝐝𝐮𝐬𝐭𝐫𝐢𝐚𝐥 𝐀𝐝𝐯𝐚𝐧𝐜𝐞𝐦𝐞𝐧𝐭 𝐄𝐱𝐡𝐢𝐛𝐢𝐭𝐢𝐨n
𝐀𝐫𝐭𝐢𝐟𝐢𝐜𝐢𝐚𝐥 𝐈𝐧𝐭𝐞𝐥𝐥𝐢𝐠𝐞𝐧𝐜𝐞 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐈𝐧𝐝𝐮𝐬𝐭𝐫𝐢𝐚𝐥 𝐀𝐝𝐯𝐚𝐧𝐜𝐞𝐦𝐞𝐧𝐭 𝐄𝐱𝐡𝐢𝐛𝐢𝐭𝐢𝐨n
𝐀𝐫𝐭𝐢𝐟𝐢𝐜𝐢𝐚𝐥 𝐈𝐧𝐭𝐞𝐥𝐥𝐢𝐠𝐞𝐧𝐜𝐞 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐈𝐧𝐝𝐮𝐬𝐭𝐫𝐢𝐚𝐥 𝐀𝐝𝐯𝐚𝐧𝐜𝐞𝐦𝐞𝐧𝐭 𝐄𝐱𝐡𝐢𝐛𝐢𝐭𝐢𝐨n Happenings At KFUEIT General News News & Updates Seminars & Conferences One Day Artificial Intelligence and Industrial Advancement Exhibition and Seminar on November 7, 2024 The Higher Education Department South Punjab proudly organized the one-day Artificial Intelligence and Industrial Advancement Exhibition and Seminar on November 7, 2024, at Multan Marquee. This landmark event brought together over twenty leading universities and educational institutions, making it a groundbreaking platform for research and innovation under the renowned Triple Helix Model. This model, which fosters collaboration between academia, industry, and government, set the stage for Pakistan’s first-ever AI and industrial innovation expo of this scale. The expo was officially inaugurated by Additional Chief Secretary Fuad Hashim Rabbani, joined by professors, industry leaders, South Punjab Special Secretaries, and numerous sponsors who united to witness this unique fusion of knowledge, technology, and industrial solutions. On the direction of our esteemed Vice Chancellor, the KFUEIT Team led by Dr. Muhammad Bilal Tahir, Provost KFUEIT, enthusiastically participated in this event, showcasing the university’s commitment to pioneering research and sustainable solutions. Our delegation included prominent faculty members such as Dr. Badar Ul Islam, Dr. Rana Mujahid, Dr. Adnan Khalil, dedicated staff, and enthusiastic students. They represented KFUEIT with pride, participating in various discussions, presentations, and networking sessions. A total of 26 innovative projects from fields like Artificial Intelligence, Biotechnology, Green Energy, Material Science, Health, and Environmental Sustainability were presented by our talented undergraduate students. These projects received high praise for their forward-thinking approaches and practical applications. Among them, the project “Resilient Recyclables: Transforming Plastic Waste into Campus Benches” garnered significant attention for its creative approach to sustainability and was a standout in both the exhibition and seminar. The interest in commercializing this project nationwide underscores its potential impact on environmental innovation and practical applications across Pakistan. The event also featured thought-provoking seminars and live demonstrations, where participants delved into the significance of these projects and explored commercialization pathways. Investors and funders engaged deeply with these projects, expressing keen interest in their development and implementation, recognizing the transformative potential of these scientific innovations. In a memorable closing ceremony, Additional Chief Secretary South Punjab honored our Vice Chancellor with a shield and presented certificates to KFUEIT’s remarkable students, celebrating their contributions to this inspiring event. This expo marks an important milestone in Pakistan's journey toward technological and industrial advancement, and KFUEIT is proud to be at the forefront of this movement. Together, we are driving innovation, sustainability, and impactful solutions for a brighter future!

A total of 26 innovative projects from fields like Artificial Intelligence, Biotechnology, Green Energy, Material Science, Health, and Environmental Sustainability were presented by our talented undergraduate students. These projects received high praise for their forward-thinking approaches and practical applications. Among them, the project “Resilient Recyclables: Transforming Plastic Waste into Campus Benches” garnered significant attention for its creative approach to sustainability and was a standout in both the exhibition and seminar. The interest in commercializing this project nationwide underscores its potential impact on environmental innovation and practical applications across Pakistan.
The event also featured thought-provoking seminars and live demonstrations, where participants delved into the significance of these projects and explored commercialization pathways. Investors and funders engaged deeply with these projects, expressing keen interest in their development and implementation, recognizing the transformative potential of these scientific innovations.
In a memorable closing ceremony, Additional Chief Secretary South Punjab honored our Vice Chancellor with a shield and presented certificates to KFUEIT’s remarkable students, celebrating their contributions to this inspiring event.
This expo marks an important milestone in Pakistan's journey toward technological and industrial advancement, and KFUEIT is proud to be at the forefront of this movement. Together, we are driving innovation, sustainability, and impactful solutions for a brighter future!

UI GreenMetric World University Rankings 2022
UI GreenMetric World University Rankings 2022 Happenings At KFUEIT General News News & Updates QEC News In UI GreenMetric World University Rankings 2022, KFUEIT rose and in top 3 positions in Pakistan. Now, the university is within top 200 resilient institutions. Besides, KFUEIT was included to three best Pakistan universities. UI GreenMetric World University Rankings determine how institutions implement the Sustainable Development Goals (infrastructure, water resource management, waste recycling, power and climate change, transport, ecological education, research). This year, 1050 organizations from 85 countries were comprised. In particular, 58 Ukrainian universities succeeded as well.

record_payload.title: https://kfueit.edu.pk/fine-arts-and-textile-design-exhibition-showcases-student-creativity-at-kfueit
record_payload.content:
Home Happenings At KFUEIT Fine Arts and Textile Design Exhibition Showcases Student Creativity at KFUEIT
Fine Arts and Textile Design Exhibition Showcases Student Creativity at KFUEIT
Fine Arts and Textile Design Exhibition Showcases Student Creativity at KFUEIT Happenings At KFUEIT Events General News News & Updates Institute of Humanities & Social Sciences News & Events Art & Project Exhibition Fine Arts and Textile Design Exhibition Showcases Student Creativity at KFUEIT Vice Chancellor Vice Chancellor Prof. Ir. Dr. Amir Azam Khan Khwaja Fareed University of Engineering and Information Technology (KFUEIT) has inaugurated a Fine Arts and Textile Design exhibition, showcasing the final projects of graduating students. The four-day exhibition features 36 final-year projects, including six by Fine Arts students and 26 by Textile Design students. Among the themes explored in the artwork are Phulkari embroidery, faded childhood memories, Mughal art, camel art, truck art, improvisational jugaad, and acid attacks. These themes captivated visitors and highlighted the students' talent and creativity. Vice Chancellor Prof. Ir. Dr. Amir Azam Khan toured the displays, interacted with students, and praised their work. He commended the originality and depth of the projects, noting that they reflect students' dedication, innovation, and strong cultural connections. Prof. Dr. Amir Azam Khan emphasized that the university remains committed to supporting and refining students' skills, promoting their work across various platforms to expand their opportunities and career prospects. Also in attendance were Associate Dean Dr. Ahmed Sohaib, Director of the Institute of Humanities and Arts Dr. Anwar Farooq, faculty members Arshad Rahmani, Muhammad Tayyab, Dr. Majid Rasheed, and a large number of students. The exhibition attracted participants from various departments, who appreciated their peers' efforts and celebrated the diverse talent at KFUEIT. The event underscores KFUEIT’s commitment to nurturing creativity, connecting students with cultural heritage, and equipping them with the skills to succeed in the modern world.

Faculty of Chemical, Civil & Environment Engineering
Home Faculties Faculty of Chemical, Civil & Environment Engineering
Faculty of Chemical, Civil & Environment Engineering Dr. Muhammad Sagir Associate Dean Email : dr.msagir@kfueit.edu.pk Welcome to the Faculty of Chemical, Civil, and Environmental Engineering at KFUEIT, where innovation, sustainability, and academic excellence converge to shape future leaders in engineering and environmental sciences. Our faculty is proud to house two dynamic departments: Institute of Chemical and Environmental Engineering This institute offers comprehensive undergraduate, postgraduate, and doctoral programs in Chemical Engineering and Environmental Sciences. Our curricula are designed to equip students with cutting-edge knowledge and practical skills, fostering expertise in areas such as green technologies, industrial processes, and environmental management. Department of Civil Engineering The Department of Civil Engineering offers BS and MS programs that prepare students to tackle the challenges of modern infrastructure development. With a strong emphasis on sustainable design, structural integrity, and urban planning, our graduates are well- equipped to contribute to the advancement of resilient and sustainable communities. At the Faculty of Chemical, Civil, and Environmental Engineering, we believe in cultivating a collaborative learning environment where students, faculty, and industry professionals work together to address global challenges. Our research initiatives, state-of-the-art laboratories, and industry partnerships provide students with opportunities for hands-on learning, innovation, and real-world impact. We invite you to explore our programs, connect with our community, and join us on a journey to create a sustainable and prosperous future. record_payload.content:
Faculty of Biological, Health Sciences & Technology
Home Faculties Faculty of Biological, Health Sciences & Technology
Faculty of Biological, Health Sciences & Technology Dr. Jallat Khan Associate Dean Email : jallat.khan@kfueit.edu.pk As an Associate Dean Faculty of Biological, Health Sciences & Technology (FBHST), I am delighted to welcome and introduce you to the faculty. The FBHST is composed of two institutes: the Institute of Health Sciences and the Institute of Biological Sciences. The Institute of Biological Sciences, formerly the Department of Biosciences established in 2019, aims to provide cutting-edge research and educational opportunities in the field of Applied Biological Sciences. Elevated to institute status in 2022, it is committed to fostering successful researchers in areas such as Biotechnology, Biochemistry, Forensic Sciences, Bioinformatics, and Microbiology. Our innovative approach equips students with a deep understanding of living systems through advanced molecular, biochemical, biotechnological, and computational methodologies. Similarly, the Institute of Health Sciences was established in 2019. The institute attained its current status on March 4, 2022, and offers Bachelor’s and Master’s degree programs in Medical Imaging Technology, Medical Laboratory Technology, Optometry, Clinical Psychology, Orthotics & Prosthetics, Aesthetics & Cosmetological Sciences, Doctor of Physical Therapy, and Healthcare Management. Together, these institutes represent a commitment to excellence in education and research, preparing our students for impactful careers in their respective fields. Faculty of Electrical and Computer Engineering
Home Faculties Faculty of Electrical and Computer Engineering
Faculty of Electrical and Computer Engineering Engr. Dr. Shahid Atiq Associate Dean Email : shahid.atiq@kfueit.edu.pk Welcome to the Khawaja Fareed UEIT, Faculty of Electrical and Computer Engineering! In our Faculty, world-class faculty mentor the next generation of engineering and science leaders. The proof thereof is the recognition of our degrees all around the world as well as the involvement and appreciation of our professors and researchers in the international scientific scene and organizations. Our students bring forward ideas with confidence in the lab, in the classroom, and beyond. Their contributions are invaluable to the strength of our research, instruction, and community. They are global citizens and engaged members of our local communities in Rahim Yar Khan and South Punjab – all in a rich, rigorous research environment that never loses the feel of a small college. At Khwaja Fareed UEIT, engineering students, faculty, staff, and alumni are driven by innovation. They make technological contributions that shape society and build the foundation for a better future. They engineer solutions that matter as part of an engine for good. As a community, we are collaborative by nature, innovative in spirit, and focused on making a difference. Together, we have built an environment for learning and discovery unlike any other. We are proud to be one of the few university faculties in the Pakistan whose students can construct a different small projects for educational purposes, getting ready to meet the challenges of the new industry of the future. Join us to create the 21st century here, in the middle of Pakistan, a livable and safe city with a colorful multicultural spirit. I hope you will peruse our website as well as our Undergraduate and Graduate Admissions websites. We offer a number of tours, events, and other opportunities (undergraduate, graduate) to learn more about our community of scholars and the depth, quality, and breadth of our programs. record_payload.content:
Home Events Autism Awareness: Early Identification for Better Outcomes
Autism Awareness: Early Identification for Better Outcomes
Autism Awareness: Early Identification for Better Outcomes Events General News Autism can be identified early through noticeable changes in speech, eye contact, and social skills. Early identification plays a crucial role in improving understanding, raising awareness, and enabling timely intervention for better developmental outcomes. This autism awareness initiative is organized by the Institute of Health Sciences , in collaboration with the Differently Abled Self Help Society (DASH) , with the aim of promoting inclusion, education, and support for individuals on the autism spectrum.

Home Faculties Faculty of Natural and Applied Sciences
Faculty of Natural and Applied Sciences Dr. Muhammad Bilal Tahir Associate Dean Email : m.bilaltahir@kfueit.edu.pk Welcome to the Faculty of Natural and Applied Sciences! Our faculty is dedicated to fostering a vibrant learning environment where students can thrive in their academic pursuits and contribute to advancements in their fields. We emphasize a balance of theoretical knowledge and practical application, empowering students to become problem-solvers and innovators. Our faculty offers a diverse range of programs that cater to the dynamic needs of science and technology. With experienced faculty members, state-of-the-art laboratories, and an array of resources, we ensure that our students are well-prepared for the challenges of the modern world. As the Associate Dean, I am committed to supporting both faculty and students in their academic journey, fostering an inclusive environment that encourages curiosity, collaboration, and excellence. We believe in the power of science to shape a better future, and I invite you to be a part of this journey with us. Best wishes for a productive and inspiring academic year! record_payload.content:
Faculty of Mechanical & Agriculture Engineering
Home Faculties Faculty of Mechanical & Agriculture Engineering
Faculty of Mechanical & Agriculture Engineering Engr. Dr. Yasir Niaz Associate Dean Email : yasir.niaz@kfueit.edu.pk I am delighted to welcome you to the Faculty of Mechanical and Agriculture Engineering. There are two departments namely the Department of Agricultural Engineering (AE) and the Institute of Mechanical and Manufacturing Engineering (M&ME). The Department of Agricultural Engineering offers BS, MS, and PhD degree programs in Agricultural Engineering and Agriculture Sciences since 2018 & 19, respectively with remarkable achievements and contributions to the innovation, and well-being of the local society since its inception in 2018. The Institute of Mechanical and Manufacturing Engineering has significantly advanced since its founding in 2014. The department offers BS, MS, and PhD Degree programs in Mechanical Engineering. Our mission is to educate, inspire, and equip the students with the technical expertise and critical thinking skills necessary to address the challenges faced by Pakistan’s agriculture and industrial sector. I am proud to lead a faculty committed to excellence in teaching, research, and innovation. I am pursuing its mission while ensuring a conducive and stimulating environment that fosters creativity, adaptability, and a sense of responsibility towards society. Together, let's harness technology, collaboration, and creative problem-solving to transform the agricultural and industrial sectors. record_payload.title: https://kfueit.edu.pk/colorful-sports-gala-commences-at-kfueit-with-enthusiastic-performances
record_payload.content:
Home General News Colorful Sports Gala Commences at KFUEIT with Enthusiastic Performances
Colorful Sports Gala Commences at KFUEIT with Enthusiastic Performances
Colorful Sports Gala Commences at KFUEIT with Enthusiastic Performances General News News & Updates Sports & Fitness The 8th Annual Sports Gala at Khwaja Fareed University of Engineering and Information Technology (KFUEIT) has officially begun, showcasing vibrant and energetic performances from students. The grand opening ceremony was inaugurated by Vice Chancellor Prof. Ir. Dr. Amir Azam Khan, alongside Pro-Vice Chancellor Prof. Dr. Shahzad Murtaza, Registrar Dr. Muhammad Javed Iqbal, and President of the District Press Club Qaisar Ghafoor. The event witnessed a warm welcome as players and organizers showered flowers upon the esteemed guests. On the first day, students from various departments participated in a march-past, saluting the stage where dignitaries were present. In his address, the Vice Chancellor emphasized the university's commitment to promoting healthy activities alongside academics. He noted that students' enthusiasm in both academics and sports is highly commendable and reflects positively on their holistic development. He highlighted that sports play a crucial role in keeping youth physically fit and socially productive, shielding them from negative influences. President Qaisar Ghafoor applauded KFUEIT for its remarkable achievements in a short span, praising the leadership of Prof. Amir Azam Khan and his team. He expressed admiration for the university's academic vibrancy, lush green campus, and well-maintained sports grounds. The first day featured thrilling competitions in tug-of-war, taekwondo, athletics, cricket, football, volleyball, and handball, with participants demonstrating exceptional talent and sportsmanship. The event was attended by prominent faculty members, including Dr. Muhammad Sagir, Dr. Basharat Gul, Dr. Adnan Bajwa, Dr. Bilal Tahir, and many others, along with a large number of students. KFUEIT remains steadfast in its mission to foster a balanced environment where education and extracurricular activities go hand in hand.

record_payload.title: https://kfueit.edu.pk/kfueit-hosts-annual-sports-gala-2025-with-grand-opening-ceremony
record_payload.content:
Home General News KFUEIT Hosts Annual Sports Gala 2025 with Grand Opening Ceremony
KFUEIT Hosts Annual Sports Gala 2025 with Grand Opening Ceremony
KFUEIT Hosts Annual Sports Gala 2025 with Grand Opening Ceremony General News News & Updates Sports & Fitness Rahim Yar Khan: The Annual Sports Gala 2025 at Khwaja Fareed University of Engineering & Information Technology (KFUEIT) was formally inaugurated in an impressive ceremony held on campus. The event was graced by Deputy Commissioner Rahim Yar Khan, Zahir Anwar Jappa, as the chief guest, while the proceedings were led under the dynamic leadership of the Vice Chancellor, Prof. Ir. Dr. Amir Azam Khan. A large gathering comprising university deans, faculty members, administrative officers, and a significant number of male and female students attended the opening ceremony, reflecting the vibrant academic and co-curricular culture of the university. Addressing the participants, the Deputy Commissioner emphasized the importance of sports in promoting positive activities among youth. He highlighted that sports not only contribute to physical fitness but also nurture discipline, teamwork, leadership, and a healthy competitive spirit. He reaffirmed that the Punjab Government and district administration would continue to extend full support for the promotion of co-curricular activities in educational institutions. He also congratulated the university administration on organizing a well-managed Sports Gala and encouraged students to actively participate in sports alongside their academic pursuits. Speaking on the occasion, Vice Chancellor Prof. Ir. Dr. Amir Azam Khan thanked the Deputy Commissioner for his presence and support. He also expressed gratitude to Mr. Sheikh Imad ud Din, CEO Khaloos Ghee, and Mian Aamir Shahbaz (JPL) for their cooperation and support in promoting sports and youth activities at the university. The Vice Chancellor underlined that KFUEIT accords special importance to sports and co-curricular activities to ensure the holistic development of students. The Vice Chancellor specially appreciated the efforts of the Sports Department and Director Sports, Dr. Basharat, for their dedicated planning, coordination, and successful organization of the Annual Sports Gala 2025. He acknowledged that the professionalism and commitment of the sports team played a key role in making the event vibrant, disciplined, and engaging for students. At the conclusion of the ceremony, the Deputy Commissioner visited various sports venues, observed different matches, and appreciated the enthusiasm and performance of the students. Commissioner Cup Sports Gala 2020 Athletics Meet 2020, Bahawalpur
Commissioner Cup Sports Gala 2020 Athletics Meet 2020, Bahawalpur Happenings At KFUEIT News Feed Sports & Fitness Congratulations to KFUEIT for 6 Gold, 2 Silver and 1 Bronze medals in Commissioner Cup Sports Gala 2020 Athletics Meet 2020 Athletes from KFUEIT represented the Rahim Yar Khan in Commissioner Cup Sports Gala 2020 Athletics Meet 2020 which was organized by Divisional Sports Department, Bahawalpur on 23rd and 24th December 2020 in Dring Stadium Bahawalpur. The students of KFUEIT won nine medals in different categories of athletics. 6 Gold Medals 2 Silver Medals 1 Bronze Medal Overall district Rahim Yar Khan got 2nd position in athletics meet 2020. Indeed, it is an outcome of the hard work of students and the dedicated effort made by the Director Sports Dr. Samina Sarwat and Sports Incharge Mr. Yasir and Coaches.

record_payload.title: https://kfueit.edu.pk/kfueit-shines-at-lahore-science-mela-2025
record_payload.content:
Home General News KFUEIT Shines at Lahore Science Mela 2025!
KFUEIT Shines at Lahore Science Mela 2025!
KFUEIT Shines at Lahore Science Mela 2025! General News News & Updates Art & Project Exhibition We are delighted to share that the students of Khwaja Fareed University of Engineering & Information Technology (KFUEIT) proudly represented the university at the Lahore Science Mela (LSM) 2025 — Pakistan’s largest and South Asia’s most vibrant citizen science festival. This mega event, organized by the Khwarizmi Science Society (KSS), attracts 100,000+ visitors and brings together schools, colleges, and universities from across the country to showcase innovation, creativity, and scientific spirit. Lahore Science Mela is a flagship initiative of Khwarizmi Science Society (KSS) — Pakistan’s oldest and most influential grassroots science awareness movement since 1996. KFUEIT students actively presented two remarkable and impactful projects: 1. Solar-Rechargeable Electric Bike A sustainable, eco-friendly mobility solution designed to reduce carbon emissions and promote green transportation. The innovation and practicality of this prototype captivated participants of all ages. 2. IoT-Based mergency Ventilator & Patient Health Monitoring System A life-saving, low-cost medical technology aimed at supporting emergency healthcare. With real-time patient monitoring and automated ventilation support, this project reflects our students’ commitment to solving real-world challenges. These exhibits received exceptional appreciation from visitors, scientists, industry representatives, and fellow exhibitors. I am immensely proud of the dedication, creativity, and hard work of our students and faculty teams for bringing such meaningful innovations to the national stage. We extend our sincere gratitude to Khwarizmi Science Society (KSS) and Dr. Muhammad Sabieh Anwar, General Secretary KSS, for extending a warm invitation to KFUEIT and appreciating the innovative projects of our students. A special thanks to Ms. Hijab Zahra, Science Communicator at KSS, for coordinating and facilitating this opportunity. Events like the Lahore Science Mela play a vital role in fostering a scientific culture in Pakistan, and KFUEIT remains committed to contributing meaningfully to such national initiatives. Proud moment for KFUEIT. Proud moment for Rahim Yar Khan. Proud moment for Pakistan. Together, let’s continue empowering our youth to innovate, create, and inspire!

Final Year Project Exhibition: Showcasing Innovation in Computer Science
Final Year Project Exhibition: Showcasing Innovation in Computer Science General News News & Updates Art & Project Exhibition The Institute of Computer Science held the Final Year Project-I Exhibition, as part of the students’ project evaluation process. Seventh-semester students of BSCS presented their projects and delivered poster presentations to attendees at the ICT Building. Faculty members conducted panel evaluations based on criteria such as idea pitching, project feasibility, business model, requirement engineering, and overall system design. Distinguished guests included Associate Dean Engr. Dr. Badar ul Islam, Director Student Affairs, Dr. Aslam Khan and Dr. Saleem Ullah. Director Institute of Computer Science, Engr. Dr. Ahmed Sohaib welcomed the guests and encouraged the students and their supervisors for their efforts. He also appreciated the efforts of FYP Coordinator Ms. Madiha Rehman and Chief Coordinator of CS Dr. Humaira Anwer in arranging the activity.

KFUEIT Hosts First Poster Exhibition 2024
KFUEIT Hosts First Poster Exhibition 2024 General News News & Updates Art & Project Exhibition The Institute of Humanities and Arts at Khwaja Fareed University of Engineering and Information Technology (KFUEIT) organized the First Poster Exhibition 2024 English Linguistics & Literature, featuring creative and academic posters prepared by BS English 1st and 2nd Semester students. The event was inaugurated by Dr. Muhammad Anwar Farooq, Director of the Institute of Humanities and Arts, alongside distinguished faculty members Dr. Shahid Habib, Dr. Majid Rasheed, and Mr. Waheed Shahzad. A large number of students and teachers attended the exhibition, appreciating the students' innovative approaches and academic insights. The event provided an excellent platform for young learners to explore and present their understanding of English Linguistics and Literature in a creative manner.

record_payload.title: https://kfueit.edu.pk/institute-of-information-technology-hosts-final-year-project-ii-exhibition-for-bs-it-students
record_payload.content:
Home General News Institute of Information Technology Hosts Final Year Project-II Exhibition for BS-IT Students
Institute of Information Technology Hosts Final Year Project-II Exhibition for BS-IT Students
Institute of Information Technology Hosts Final Year Project-II Exhibition for BS-IT Students General News News & Updates Art & Project Exhibition The Institute of Information Technology held the Final Year Project-II Exhibition as part of the students' external project evaluation process. Eighth-semester students of BS-IT presented their projects and delivered poster presentations to attendees at the ICT Building. External evaluators conducted panel evaluations based on criteria such as design and implementation, modern tools, impact on society, professional ethics, project management and teamwork, presentation, and documentation. Distinguished guests included Zia Ahmed, CEO of GenWizz Solutions; Muhammad Nabil, founder of Semantic Solutions; Abdul Rehman, CEO at Semantic Solutions; and Umer Shafique, CEO of Promotezz International, who served as external evaluators. Faculty members from the IT department were also in attendance, including Dr. Madiha Amjad, Mr. Aqeel Ur Rehman, Dr. Muhammad Rizwan, Mr. Syed Ali Jafar Zaidi, and Mr. Muhammad Adeen Riaz. Dr. Kashif Munir, in-charge of the Institute of Information Technology, welcomed the guests and encouraged the students and their supervisors for their efforts. He also appreciated the efforts of the FYP coordinator and advisor to the ITEC Society of IT, Mr. Muhammad Adeen Riaz, in arranging the activity.

record_payload.title: https://kfueit.edu.pk/kfueit-hosts-impressive-degree-show-by-textile-design-and-fine-arts-students
record_payload.content:
Home General News KFUEIT Hosts Impressive Degree Show by Textile Design and Fine Arts Students
KFUEIT Hosts Impressive Degree Show by Textile Design and Fine Arts Students
KFUEIT Hosts Impressive Degree Show by Textile Design and Fine Arts Students General News News & Updates Art & Project Exhibition Rahim Yar Khan (18-11-2025 ) The Department of Textile Designing and Fine Arts at Khwaja Fareed University of Engineering and Information Technology (KFUEIT) organized an impressive exhibition showcasing the final-year projects of its students. Held under the title Degree Show, the event featured the creative work of 36 Textile Design students and 5 Fine Arts students, all developed under the supervision of Arshad Rehmani and Tayyab Raza. The exhibition was inaugurated by Vice Chancellor Prof. Dr. Amir Azam Khan. Registrar Dr. Javed Iqbal, Director ORIC Dr. Saif-ur-Rehman, Director Legal Mian Irfan, Director Institute of Humanities Dr. Muhammad Anwar Farooq, along with a large number of faculty members and students, attended the ceremony. The artworks presented by the students remained the center of attention throughout the event. Vice Chancellor Prof. Dr. Amir Azam Khan praised the outstanding abilities of the students and faculty, stating that the Degree Show is a testament to their exceptional talent. He emphasized that KFUEIT is not limited to engineering and technology; rather, it is equally committed to promoting arts, humanities, and fine arts. The exhibition featured a diverse collection of traditional and modern textile designs, paintings, sketches, and sculptures crafted from various materials. The displayed artworks not only reflected the students’ creative vision but also beautifully showcased the blend of contemporary trends with cultural heritage. Each piece highlighted harmony in colors, meticulous detailing, and deep conceptual thought.

record_payload.title: https://kfueit.edu.pk/kfueit-observes-international-day-of-the-girl-child-with-special-seminar
record_payload.content:
Home Events KFUEIT Observes International Day of the Girl Child with Special Seminar
KFUEIT Observes International Day of the Girl Child with Special Seminar
KFUEIT Observes International Day of the Girl Child with Special Seminar Events General News Health and Safety Rahim Yar Khan, October 11, 2024 – Khwaja Fareed University of Engineering and Information Technology (KFUEIT) organized a special seminar in observance of the International Day of the Girl Child. The event, held under the Directorate of Student Affairs, featured Dr. M. Anwar Farooq, Director of the Institute of Humanities and Arts, as the guest speaker. In his address, Dr. Anwar Farooq highlighted key issues impacting girls around the world, focusing on three main topics: the importance of education, healthcare, and protection from violence and exploitation. “Education is the key to unlocking the potential of girls and creating a more equitable society,” said Dr. Farooq, urging students and faculty to advocate for quality education for girls everywhere. He also emphasized the need for accessible healthcare, stating, “Girls must have access to healthcare services that ensure their physical and mental well-being.” Finally, he discussed the urgent need for stronger measures to protect girls from violence and exploitation, adding, “We must create safe environments where girls can grow up without fear of harm.” Vice Chancellor Prof. Ir. Dr. Amir Azam Khan, in his remarks, reaffirmed KFUEIT's commitment to societal progress. "At KFUEIT, we believe in nurturing an inclusive environment where every individual, regardless of gender, can achieve their full potential. Our institution stands firmly with initiatives that uplift and empower girls and women,” he stated. The seminar aimed to raise awareness among students and faculty, inspiring them to become advocates for gender equality and the rights of girls in society.

In his address, Dr. Anwar Farooq highlighted key issues impacting girls around the world, focusing on three main topics: the importance of education, healthcare, and protection from violence and exploitation. “Education is the key to unlocking the potential of girls and creating a more equitable society,” said Dr. Farooq, urging students and faculty to advocate for quality education for girls everywhere. He also emphasized the need for accessible healthcare, stating, “Girls must have access to healthcare services that ensure their physical and mental well-being.” Finally, he discussed the urgent need for stronger measures to protect girls from violence and exploitation, adding, “We must create safe environments where girls can grow up without fear of harm.”
Vice Chancellor Prof. Ir. Dr. Amir Azam Khan, in his remarks, reaffirmed KFUEIT's commitment to societal progress. "At KFUEIT, we believe in nurturing an inclusive environment where every individual, regardless of gender, can achieve their full potential. Our institution stands firmly with initiatives that uplift and empower girls and women,” he stated.
The seminar aimed to raise awareness among students and faculty, inspiring them to become advocates for gender equality and the rights of girls in society.

record_payload.content:
Home General News International Relations Expo 2025: Culture as Diplomacy
International Relations Expo 2025: Culture as Diplomacy
International Relations Expo 2025: Culture as Diplomacy General News News & Updates Art & Project Exhibition On 26 November 2025 , the International Relations Society (IRS) at Khwaja Fareed University of Engineering and Information Technology (KFUEIT) successfully organized the International Relations Expo 2025 under the theme “Culture as Diplomacy: How Traditions Shape International Relations and Cooperation.” The expo featured representations of 12 countries —China, Pakistan, Turkey, Iran, Saudi Arabia, Palestine, Uzbekistan, Qatar, the United States, Germany, Ethiopia, and Brazil—presented through immersive and interactive student-led stalls highlighting their cultural heritage, traditions, and diplomatic significance. The event was honored by the presence of Pro Vice Chancellor Dr. Shehzad Murtaza , Director IHA Dr. Muhammad Anwar Farooq , Advisor IRS Mr. Ehsan Ali , Additional Controller Examination Mr. Yasir Arafat , along with esteemed faculty members including Dr. Mehwish Muzaffar, Mr. Haris Kabir, Mr. Fazzal Abbas, Dr. Ghulam Qasim, Ms. Misbah Wahid, Mr. Zain-ul-Abedeen Arshad, Dr. Majid Rasheed, and Dr. Mazhar Hussain . The dignitaries commended the organizers and participants for their dedication and exceptional execution. By effectively combining academic learning with active engagement, the International Relations Expo 2025 promoted global awareness, cross-cultural understanding, and experiential learning, marking a significant achievement for the International Relations Society at KFUEIT.

Home Happenings At KFUEIT First Exhibition of Semester Projects in Computer Engineering (ESPiCE)
First Exhibition of Semester Projects in Computer Engineering (ESPiCE)
First Exhibition of Semester Projects in Computer Engineering (ESPiCE) Happenings At KFUEIT Art & Project Exhibition department of computer engineering has organized its first espice on march 1, 2018, in civil building kfueit. we have introduced espice to gather all the departments to showcase their projects work with all the university. the espice brought together students from many departments such as computer sciences, electrical engineering and civil engineering under one umbrella to showcase their talent in the form of different projects. we got 22 groups from bscs 1st section a and b (supervised by engr. dr. naeem maroof and co-supervised by engr. zainab saleem), 12 groups from bscs 1st section c (supervised by engr. hammad shahab), 18 groups from bs electrical engineering (supervised by engr. abdul basit and engr. muhammad kamran) and 4 groups from bs civil engineering (supervised by engr. dr. hamza ahmad madni and co-supervised by mr. hamail ayaz). the students displayed their work with so much eagerness the whole day in the meanwhile our respected judges evaluated the students. the closing ceremony started with recitation of holy quran and followed by remarkable speeches from worth vice chancellor engr. prof. dr. athar mahboob and head of department computer engineering, engr. ahmad sohaib. in the last positions were announced for the first 3 best projects. 1st position -> project title "cricket mania" supervised by engr. dr. naeem maroof and co-supervised by engr. zainab saleem bscs 1st (b), talha asghar, muhammad junaid nawab, muhammad badar and abdul basit 2nd position -> project title "pizza ordering system using c++ and vb" supervised by engr. hammad shahab bscs 1st (c) faizan qadeer, kiran yaqoob, kainat saleem and rakshanda qasim 3rd position -> project title "electrical installation and working cycle analyzer" supervised by engr. abdul basit bs electrical engineering 2nd semester anusha fatima, rizwan zafar, waqas khan and nida tahir link for reference http://cpe.kfueit.edu.pk/index.php/news-events/92-first-espice-exhibition-of-semester-projects-in-computer-engineering

record_payload.title: https://kfueit.edu.pk/kfueit-hosts-50th-international-seerat-conference
record_payload.content:
Home General News KFUEIT Hosts 50th International Seerat Conference
KFUEIT Hosts 50th International Seerat Conference
KFUEIT Hosts 50th International Seerat Conference General News News & Updates Seminars & Conferences RAHIM YAR KHAN 03-09-2025– In observance of the birth anniversary of Holy Prophet, Hazrat Muhammad (PBUH), Khwaja Fareed University of Engineering & Information Technology (KFUEIT), Rahim Yar Khan, held its 50th International Seerat Conference today at the university's Civil Auditorium. The conference, which featured distinguished speakers from Pakistan and abroad, was attended in person and online. Vice Chancellor Prof. Ir. Dr. Aamir Azam Khan welcomed the esteemed guests, including Mian Amir Shahbaz, emphasizing the importance of following the Prophet's teachings. "Only by following the Seerat-un-Nabi ﷺ can we transform this world into a peaceful, stable, and prosperous society," he stated. He also highlighted the university's fundamental responsibility to educate students about the Prophet's life to build a society aligned with his exemplary conduct. The conference's theme was "State Responsibilities in Educating and Training for the Positive Use of Social Media in the Light of Seerat-un-Nabi ﷺ." Prominent scholars from Pakistan, Egypt, and Malaysia participated online, highlighting the role of social media in promoting the timeless teachings of the Prophet (PBUH). Speakers at the event included Dr. Muhammad Muslim (Khwaja Fareed College, Rahim Yar Khan), Dr. Atif Aslam Rao (University of Karachi), Prof. Dr. Muhammad Zia-ul-Haq (International Islamic University, Islamabad), Dr. Khalid Abdul Razzaq (Jamia Al Azhar, Cairo), Dr. Halim Ismail (International Islamic University Malaysia), and Khalifa Peer Qari Abdul Rehman Naqshbandi (Rahim Yar Khan), who addressed various topics related to the theme. The conference aimed to help attendees understand how the Prophet's teachings can guide humanity in the face of social media's growing influence.

record_payload.content:
Home General News Two-Day Workshop on Entrepreneurial Ecosystems Successfully Concludes at KFUEIT
Two-Day Workshop on Entrepreneurial Ecosystems Successfully Concludes at KFUEIT
Two-Day Workshop on Entrepreneurial Ecosystems Successfully Concludes at KFUEIT General News News & Updates Seminars & Conferences Khwaja Fareed University of Engineering and Information Technology (KFUEIT) successfully hosted a Two-Day Training Workshop on Entrepreneurial Ecosystems, led by Prof. Dr. Shahid Qureshi, Rector of GIFT University Gujranwala. Upon his arrival, Dr. Qureshi was warmly welcomed by Worthy Vice Chancellor Prof. Ir. Dr. Amir Azam Khan, who appreciated his exceptional contributions to entrepreneurship and innovation. Dr. Qureshi, a renowned expert and entrepreneur, shared invaluable insights on building and sustaining entrepreneurial ecosystems. His sessions covered essential topics such as cultivating an entrepreneurial mindset, developing innovative business models, and crafting effective startup strategies. Participants engaged in interactive discussions, hands-on activities, and Q&A sessions, gaining practical knowledge to navigate the entrepreneurial landscape. The workshop concluded with a certificate distribution ceremony, recognizing the dedication and active participation of attendees. This initiative underscores KFUEIT’s commitment to empowering its academic community and fostering a culture of entrepreneurship and innovation.

Home General News KFUEIT Champions Global Scholarships: Seminar Highlights Erasmus Mundus as a Top Opportunity for Pakistani Students
KFUEIT Champions Global Scholarships: Seminar Highlights Erasmus Mundus as a Top Opportunity for Pakistani Students
KFUEIT Champions Global Scholarships: Seminar Highlights Erasmus Mundus as a Top Opportunity for Pakistani Students General News News & Updates Seminars & Conferences RAHIM YAR KHAN, PUNJAB – November 4, 2025 – Khwaja Fareed University of Engineering and Information Technology (KFUEIT) successfully hosted a crucial awareness seminar on the Erasmus Mundus Master Scholarship Program 2026-28, aimed at preparing final-year undergraduate students for this prestigious international opportunity. The seminar was jointly organized by the Office of Research, Innovation, and Commercialization (ORIC), in collaboration with the Directorate of Career Counseling and the Directorate of Academics. The keynote speaker for the event was Engr. Dr. M. Saif Ur Rehman, Director ORIC, himself a distinguished Erasmus Mundus Alumni. Dr. Rehman addressed students from the 6th to 8th semesters, providing a comprehensive overview of the scholarship's immense importance, detailed structure, admission procedure, and necessary documentation. Pakistan's Edge in Global Competition Dr. Rehman highlighted the exceptional competitiveness of KFUEIT students and their peers across the nation, stating that the Erasmus Mundus Master Scholarship is one of the most exciting and prestigious opportunities available. “The most significant point for our students is that for the last two consecutive years, Pakistani students have secured the maximum number of these fully-funded scholarships globally, surpassing formidable competitors like India and China,” stated Dr. Rehman. “This performance underscores the high academic potential and dedication present in our youth, and KFUEIT is committed to ensuring our students seize these opportunities.” Strategic Preparation is Key to Success During the session, Dr. Rehman strongly emphasized the necessity of strategic, long-term preparation. He advised undergraduates to commence their scholarship preparations at least one year before the application deadline to meet all stringent eligibility criteria. Key areas of focus highlighted for maximizing application success included: Achieving competitive scores in English language proficiency requirements. Thoroughly researching and understanding their programs of interest and mobility pathways. Defining clear future goals. Dr. Rehman particularly stressed the critical role of the Letter of Motivation, advising students that it must meticulously establish a strong, clear correlation between their current field of study, skills gained, practical experience (including publications or industrial internships), and their proposed future career objectives. The seminar concluded with a highly interactive question-and-answer session, reinforcing KFUEIT's commitment to fostering a culture of global academic ambition and excellence among its student body.

record_payload.content:

KFUEIT Workshop Pushes for Green Technology and Interdisciplinary Solutions to Tackle Industrial Pollution
KFUEIT Workshop Pushes for Green Technology and Interdisciplinary Solutions to Tackle Industrial Pollution General News News & Updates Seminars & Conferences RAHIM YAR KHAN, [Date of Workshop] – Khwaja Fareed University of Engineering and Information Technology (KFUEIT) successfully concluded a consultative workshop focused on the critical relationship between technological innovation and environmental pollution, organized under the prestigious HEC NRPU Project No. 20-14732. The workshop, titled "Asymmetric impact of technological innovation on environmental pollution in Pakistan using quantile estimation," brought together research scholars from KFUEIT and key representatives from the local industry to discuss urgent environmental challenges and propose policy-level solutions. The event was a collaborative effort by the Office of Research, Innovation and Commercialization (ORIC) KFUEIT, HEC, and the Institute of Business Administration (IBA) KFUEIT. Dr. Adnan Maqbool, Principal Investigator (PI) of the project, presented his research findings to the participants. Dr. Maqbool highlighted the existence of various technologies capable of mitigating environmental pollution originating from industrial sources. He underscored the necessity of robust policy interventions to minimize the current pollution load, ultimately advocating for a mandatory shift of industrial technology towards green technology adoption across Pakistan. The workshop also featured a compelling online address from Dr. Muhammad Hafeez of the Institute of Business Management Sciences (IBMS), University of Agriculture. Dr. Hafeez emphasized the vital role of eco-innovation as the primary strategy for managing global environmental challenges. Furthermore, he elaborated on several financial initiatives and incentives being offered by the Government of Pakistan aimed at facilitating the widespread adoption and improvement of green technologies within the industrial landscape. Summing up the day’s discussions, Engr. Dr. Muhammad Saif Ur Rehman, Director ORIC KFUEIT, provided crucial context. He stressed that environmental pollution is a shared global challenge that cannot be solved by individual entities. He argued that an interdisciplinary approach—combining engineering, policy, and finance—is absolutely necessary to propose valid and workable technological solutions for the industry. Dr. Rehman further highlighted a key constraint: "Any innovative technology, regardless of its efficacy, cannot be scaled up without comprehensive financial integration and rigorous enforcement of environmental regulations." The consultative workshop concluded with a formal session where the Worthy Vice Chancellor presented souvenirs to the resource persons in recognition of their valuable contributions and distributed certificates to the participating industrial representatives. The event successfully fostered a strong dialogue between academia and industry, setting a clear agenda for future research and policy development in the area of environmental sustainability.
